name: Wget

core_modules.name: DOWNLOAD_ENGINE
core_modules.title: Recursive Retrieval & Mirroring Engine
core_modules.description: Enables mirroring of websites, file downloads with resume capability, and proxy authentication support.

scenarios.name: Test Scenarios & Commands
objectiveList: 5. Test Scenarios & Commands
Explanation of Command Parameters
"Server" - Web server software
scenarios.script:
wget -q https://www.google.com/robots.txt -O - | grep -v "#" | grep "/"
wget -q https://example.com -O - | grep -i "<meta" | sort
wget -q https://developer.mozilla.org -O - | grep -o "<!--.*-->" | sort | uniq
wget --server-response --spider https://www.github.com 2>&1 | grep -i "Server\|X-"
wget -q https://wordpress.org -O - | grep -i "generator"
wget -q https://www.nytimes.com -O - | grep -o "<script src=\".*\.js\"" | cut -d'"' -f2
wget -q https://www.wikipedia.org -O - | grep -o "href=\"[^\"]*.\"" | cut -d'"' -f2
wget -q https://opensource.org -O - | grep -i "author\|created\|copyright\|owner"
wget --server-response --spider https://www.reddit.com 2>&1 | grep -i "Server\|X-Powered\|X-AspNet\|X-Runtime"
wget: A utility for retrieving content from web servers
wget --server-response --spider https://www.cloudflare.com 2>&1 | grep -i "WAF\|Cloudflare\|Imperva\|Akamai\|ModSecurity"
wget --server-response --spider https://www.twitter.com 2>&1 | grep -i "Balance\|proxy\|nginx\|haproxy"

keyTakeaways.content: Reddit Server Response Headers

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_FINDING
keyTakeaways.content: Header Analysis

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_IMPLICATIONS
keyTakeaways.content: The server doesn't disclose specific software or versions, which is a good security practice that prevents attackers from targeting known vulnerabilities

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_FINDING
keyTakeaways.content: Error message analysis

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_FINDING
keyTakeaways.content: Behavioral analysis through specific requests

attackPaths.title: SECURITY_IMPLICATIONS
attackPaths.desc: The server doesn't disclose specific software or versions, which is a good security practice that prevents attackers from targeting known vulnerabilities

attackPaths.title: LOW
attackPaths.desc: With only "snooserv" as information, an attacker cannot determine specific server software or versions

attackPaths.title: HIGH
attackPaths.desc: Cloudflare presents several challenges for attackers:

scenarios.name: Test for common NGINX misconfigurations
objectiveList: Test for common NGINX misconfigurations
Attempt path traversal or unauthorized directory access
Check for accessible configuration files (nginx.conf)

keyTakeaways.title: OPERATIONAL_EFFECTIVENESS
keyTakeaways.content: Automated execution profiles of Wget accelerate baseline information gathering.

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_EXPOSURE
keyTakeaways.content: Exposing target systems configurations through OSINT indicators helps plan subsequent exploits.

keyTakeaways.title: REMEDIATION_PRIORITY
keyTakeaways.content: Patching exposed vulnerabilities immediately restricts the available entry points for attackers.

attackPaths.title: CVE_EXPLOITATION
attackPaths.desc: Target identified service version vulnerabilities with publicly available public exploits.

attackPaths.title: CREDENTIAL_SPRAYING
attackPaths.desc: Leverage exposed logins or portals to perform low-and-slow default password brute forcing.

attackPaths.title: DATA_EXFILTRATION
attackPaths.desc: Utilize open directory listings or exposed databases to directly siphon sensitive business assets.

scenarios.name: Test for SSI (Server Side Includes) injection if enabled
scenarios.objective: Test for SSI (Server Side Includes) injection if enabled
objectiveList: Test for SSI (Server Side Includes) injection if enabled
Look for custom error pages that might leak information
Remove or obscure server version information
scenarios.script:
wget -q https://www.mysql.com -O - | grep -i "sql\|oracle\|mysql\|postgres\|mongodb\|database error"
wget -q https://www.google.com/favicon.ico
wget: Utility for non-interactive downloading of files
wget --server-response --save-cookies cookies.txt --keep-session-cookies https://www.wikipedia.com 2>&1 | grep -i "Set-Cookie"
wget --server-response --save-cookies cookies.txt --keep-session-cookies https://www.wikipedia.com 2>&1 | grep -i "Set-Cookie"
wget: Web content retrieval utility

scenarios.name: Test impact of manipulating GeoIP cookie values
scenarios.objective: Test impact of manipulating GeoIP cookie values
objectiveList: Test impact of manipulating GeoIP cookie values
Determine if geo-restricted content can be accessed by cookie modification
Investigate relationship between actual IP and cookie-stored location
scenarios.script:
wget -q $(wget -q https://jquery.com -O - | grep -o "<script src=\".*\.js\"" | cut -d'"' -f2) -O - | grep -i "api\|endpoint\|config\|server\|database"
Extracts JavaScript file URLs (grep -o "<script src=\".*\.js\"" | cut -d'"' -f2)

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_FINDING
keyTakeaways.content: This analysis reveals that Wikipedia implements reasonably secure cookie practices with appropriate security flags, while providing insight into their tracking, geolocation, and network security mechanisms.

scenarios.name: Troubleshooting
objectiveList: 6. Troubleshooting
Use -v for verbose (less than debug) output
Check the server response with --server-response
scenarios.script: Use -d or --debug for verbose debugging output

scenarios.name: Security Considerations
objectiveList: 7. Security Considerations
Default User-Agent string identifies as Wget
Recursive downloads can be noisy and leave obvious patterns in logs

troubleshooting.cause: The target website uses a self-signed or invalid SSL certificate.

security: Ensure all execution requests with Wget are performed within authorized scope. Log all output artifacts securely and protect the integrity of local credential wordlists.
